blogs.blogTitle: Maximizing Productivity via Local Hiring
blogs.blogSummary: As more and more people work from home and economies throughout the world grow interconnected, companies are increasingly turning to the global online labor market to fill open positions. Modern tools make it easier for companies of all sizes to find and hire the best candidates.
blogs.blogDescp:
As more and more people work from home and economies throughout the world grow interconnected, companies are increasingly turning to the global online labor market to fill open positions. Modern tools make it easier for companies of all sizes to find and hire the best candidates.


Unfortunately, this brand-new gizmo ignores a crucial fact. Nonetheless, studies have shown that regional hiring improves economic development, worker happiness, and productivity. Local hiring is often better hiring, to put it plainly.


Workers Love Local Businesses


There are a lot of advantages for people to work locally. 

Without a lengthy commute, one of the key advantages is apparent. Employees who are required to travel lengthy distances to go to work often express less happiness with their jobs and are less productive overall. No matter where they live, having easy access to the workplace makes everyone happier.


Problems with engagement and buy-in are also possible. Local personnel may be more tuned in and invested in the company's culture and operational goals than their distant counterparts, such as those checking in from the UK.


Personnel based in the area are familiar with the difficulties faced by the community. Customer service, employee happiness, and work quality may all take a boost when employees are well-versed in the local economy and can use that knowledge to better serve their local clientele. If you're excellent at what you do for a living, it makes you happy.


Local Hiring Made Easy with a Staffing Platform


1. Use integrated Google Maps to guarantee efficiency


It is very recommended to hire folks in the area... nonetheless, which method would be most effective? 

Using the integrated Google Maps feature, you can see a prospect's proximity to the office and plot their journey to work in advance of hiring them using Jobcart's staffing platform. Aside from helping with shift planning and personnel schedule organization, being able to convey this distance to employees may be a selling point when recruiting new staff.


Jobcart's recruiting platform also provides a map view for companies to see potential prospects. When you're searching for prospects, you'll be able to see their locations superimposed. Disappearances without explanation are a major issue in unskilled labor. Worker reliability and operational excellence are both enhanced by shorter travel, as employees are less inclined to skip shifts.


2. JIT recruiting


Among the many benefits of recruiting locally is the ability to use just-in-time employment strategies. When unexpectedly tight deadlines occur and you need extra hands, local labor might be a lifesaver. There are many qualified individuals in the area who are eager to work and may start right away on Jobcart's staffing platform. 


Due to the fact that each candidate's credentials are viewable on their profile and location, hiring locally sourced personnel may decrease training and onboarding time. A streamlined onboarding procedure is possible since you may recruit workers with the necessary training already in place. Consider the Christmas season: demand skyrockets and a local crew is there to assist you in all the madness!


3. High-tech personnel scheduling


Managing your local personnel will be your next major challenge after maximizing their potential. While the Jobcart staffing platform does allow you to finish the recruiting process, that is not all it can do. Get everything done for workforce management and scheduling in one convenient location instead of juggling different apps. 


Manage the procedures after accessing a candidate's shift availability by offering shifts. Streamline your payroll processes and manage employee timesheets online using Jobcart's staffing platform. Easy operations are within your reach with a comprehensive management solution. 


Centralized Staff Members Lead to Worldwide Achievement


People are more productive and like working locally, according to economic research. The numbers speak for themselves. Jobs, whether temporary or permanent, created by local businesses should, if feasible, be filled by locals. 


Labor productivity rises in correlation with worker happiness, according to the research. You may help the economy expand and create jobs by taking advantage of new technology that makes both the job of your new recruits and their hiring managers easier and more efficient. 


Here we'll take a look at why it's a good idea to hire people from the area's labor market and how a software platform can help you find the most qualified candidates from that pool.

blogs.blogTitle: Maximizing Your Benefits from the Fourth Industrial Revolution
blogs.blogSummary: We've gone so far that we can now identify four separate epochs of revolutionary development and invention as the catalysts for our current state of affairs. We are now in the midst of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, the culmination of a series of periods that built upon one another.
blogs.blogDescp:
There has been much progress since the first industrial revolution in the 1700s. 


We've gone so far that we can now identify four separate epochs of revolutionary development and invention as the catalysts for our current state of affairs. We are now in the midst of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, the culmination of a series of periods that built upon one another.


In the early 2000s, a confluence of factors—including the rapid development of digital technology, the portability of mobile communications, and the increasing automation and transformation of corporate processes—led to the Fourth Industrial Revolution, which has since shaken up many sectors. A phrase that adequately describes this change is "disruption," because it is altering the fundamental character of both established and emerging companies.


We get here, but how?


  • In the 1700s, steam-powered equipment was introduced, marking the beginning of the First Industrial Revolution. This innovation greatly improved urbanization and replaced hand-made manufacturing with assembly-line mass production. Industrialization caused a mass exodus from the fields, the expansion of urban centers, and a subsequent alteration of the global landscape.
  • An explosion in spending and consumption accompanied by a slew of scientific breakthroughs made possible by the widespread availability of electricity in the nineteenth century ushered in the Second Industrial Revolution. Producers worked around the clock to keep production lines moving in order to fulfill consumer demand.
  • As part of the Third Industrial Revolution, the internet, advanced computers, and software all emerged in the 20th century, allowing for the development of more powerful tools. People were able to increase their consumption at a dizzying pace because of automation, which also created a massive data warehouse.  
  • We are being swept away by a wave of technological upheavals that can only be described as the Fourth Industrial Revolution. These waves include developments in quantum computing, real-time data, global telecommunications, smart manufacturing, blockchain technology, 3D printing, energy capture and delivery, and big data. 


Where we're heading?


A digital technology tune-up is exactly what certain industries have been waiting for after standing motionless for more than half a century. There is a steady stream of new capabilities being added to smart factories and machines, which are gradually replacing the production processes of the past century. Traditional workers are being supplemented by autonomous robots.


Once upon a time, only the biggest corporations could afford cutting-edge technology. Technology has grown in importance in the current corporate world, and now it's within reach of organizations of all sizes. In addition to reshaping manufacturing procedures, this technology revolution is influencing new approaches to human resource management.


Conventional methods of staffing, which were effective in the 1800s, are now completely out of date and ineffective in the modern day. To put it simply, even in a 4.0 environment, tactics from 2.0 are still being used to hire and oversee employees. Employing innovative hiring methods in tandem with cutting-edge technology gives progressive businesses a chance to outperform their competitors that are stuck in the past.


Taking Advantage of the Revolution


With the advent of the Fourth Industrial Revolution came great promise and formidable obstacles. From the perspective of human resource management, technological advancements are causing certain job categories to vanish, while others are evolving to meet the demands of the modern economy. 


New methods of supplying the labor force are necessitating fundamental shifts in the way we manufacture, trade, transport, and consume commodities. Traditional approaches are completely ineffective in this new era of upheaval. 


Businesses are currently devising novel approaches. Companies in the modern day are gaining and keeping an edge in the marketplace by using flexible staffing tactics, just-in-time hiring, temporary labor, temp-to-hire, and other alternatives to the typical full-time workforce. 


Jobcart, a digital staffing agency, is using the innovations of the Fourth Industrial Revolution (such as artificial intelligence, quantum and cloud computing, machine learning, and big data analytics) to implement these new strategies for hiring and managing employees once they're on the job.


We have entered the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Opportunities and challenges abound at the moment. Businesses may prosper in this change by using new tactics and technology.
blogs.pageTitle: Maximizing Your Benefits from the Fourth Industrial Revolution
blogs.pageKeywords: Maximizing Your Benefits from the Fourth Industrial Revolution
blogs.pageDescp: Maximizing Your Benefits from the Fourth Industrial Revolution

blogs.blogTitle: What Social Media Can Do for the Recruitment Process
blogs.blogSummary: Through both personal and professional networks, social media has revolutionized our way of connecting with others and the world around us. When it comes to hiring new employees, social media has also become an essential tool for our companies.
blogs.blogDescp:
Through both personal and professional networks, social media has revolutionized our way of connecting with others and the world around us. When it comes to hiring new employees, social media has also become an essential tool for our companies. 


Even though LinkedIn has long been linked with professional material, other social media sites like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter are seeing a rise in recruitment-related activity as well. These social media platforms provide businesses with effective methods of finding and hiring top talent, from sourcing qualified applicants to conducting preliminary interviews.


Recruiting Employees Using Social Media


A broader and more precise net


Finding new workers is a prime example of the staffing benefits of social media. Compared to more conventional forms of advertising for open positions, such as job boards and internal posts, businesses may reach a much larger audience using social media platforms such as LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter.


More than 2.9 billion people will be using Facebook in the year 2023. You have a massive pond to cast a very precise net into, thanks to Facebook's ability to target advertising by demographics, geography, and hobbies, and that enormous pool of possible workers.


Talent acquisition professionals in Calgary, for instance, may target Facebook ads to residents in the southwest sector of the city rather than randomly placing job ads throughout the whole city in the hopes that some of them would be relevant. They may also narrow their targeting to members of the interest group Forklift Drivers in Calgary's Beltline neighborhood who are between the ages of 18 and 35.


Constructing the emblem


Prospective employees form strong impressions of you based on your reputation as an employer. Showing off your company's values, purpose, and culture is a lot more effective than posting a "help wanted" ad when it comes to selling the advantages of working for your firm.


Modern HR pros know that social media is a great way to boost their employer brand. They use it to announce workplace outings like BBQs and picnics, highlight employees' successes, promote internal possibilities, and highlight the firm's values and charity work. Because of the good impact on corporate culture, these businesses are in higher demand among job-seekers.


Uninvolved applicants


Not everyone who uses social media is actively looking for a job. Quite a few top-tier applicants are already working and have no interest in leaving their existing positions... would be open to considering a new opportunity if it were to present itself in an ideal way. Opportunities may be discovered by fresh talent who isn't necessarily using job search websites when they have an active social media presence.


Staff recommendations


Your present staff may easily spread the word about openings in your company to their personal and professional networks thanks to social media. And since your staff already know these individuals, you have a better chance of hiring them because they will be a perfect match for your company.


Choosing the Appropriate One


An important component of creating a cohesive team and keeping talent is finding individuals who are a good match with a company's culture. You may learn a lot about a candidate's character, beliefs, and hobbies from their social media profiles, which can help you assess whether they are a good fit for your company's culture.


However, one must exercise caution when using social media for this function, since it is possible to allow unconscious prejudices to impact the selection process. There is a large gap between the online and offline selves of many individuals, especially when it comes to social media and the job.


They Will Show Up If You Construct It


Although using preexisting social media platforms is nothing new for employment pros, some forward-thinking firms are taking it a notch further by integrating social media features into their own software. One such pioneer is Jobcart, a digital employment service.


According to Ajay Shokar, owner of Jobcart, "Users of Jobcart can leverage social media right within our app to innovate the job application process." Jobcart allows users to "like" jobs, follow businesses, and share job openings on major social media platforms like as Facebook, Twitter, WhatsApp, LinkedIn, and more.


In addition to being able to connect with other social media sites, Jobcart also has its own built-in networking features.


With Jobcart, users can connect with one another, communicate with employers through instant messaging and connection requests, form communities of people who share interests, preferences, and skill sets, and showcase their education, work experience, and abilities to potential employers through video profiles, according to Ajay. "Think of it as LinkedIn for factory workers."


Simply put, Social Media


Recruiters used to have to just throw their nets wide open and hope that good people would swim through. These days, thanks to social media, they can pinpoint exactly where the fish are hiding and cast their line at them.


Social media has the potential to revolutionize how organizations attract candidates in today's competitive job market—as long as they use it ethically and with the correct strategy. Human resources departments now have a potent weapon at their disposal—social hiring—to locate, assess, and recruit exceptional candidates.
blogs.pageTitle: What Social Media Can Do for the Recruitment Process

blogs.blogTitle: Artificial Intelligence and Its Impact on the Employment Industry
blogs.blogSummary: Nearly every aspect of our digital life now incorporates some kind of artificial intelligence (AI). You already encounter AI on a daily basis in many forms: social media, internet searches, voice assistants (Siri or Alexa), maps (Google Maps), online banking, Amazon, Netflix, and shopping.
blogs.blogDescp:
Nearly every aspect of our digital life now incorporates some kind of artificial intelligence (AI). You already encounter AI on a daily basis in many forms: social media, internet searches, voice assistants (Siri or Alexa), maps (Google Maps), online banking, Amazon, Netflix, and shopping.


The recruiting profession has always been about humans seeking employment for other people, unlike the digital experiences mentioned before. Although this core idea remains the same, artificial intelligence has greatly enhanced every step of the recruiting and staffing process, including but not limited to recruitment, workforce management, payroll processing, automation of corporate processes, and more. The staffing sector is seeing a transformation as a consequence. 


Artificial intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing personnel management and the recruitment process in several ways.


Strengthened candidate sourcing


AI has the capacity to sift through mountains of resumes and sort them in order of merit. Data from personality profiles, professional networks, and social media may also be included. Recruiters can find the best candidates for open positions by analyzing applicant data (including their abilities, experience, education, and personality traits) using machine learning algorithms.


Recruiters no longer have to do as much tedious physical labor thanks to AI-powered sourcing. Because of this, they are able to spend more time developing meaningful relationships with both businesses and prospects, which ultimately leads to better placements.


Matchmaking done by machines


Tools powered by artificial intelligence may sift through resumes and job postings to find the best possible applicants. Factors such as a candidate's personality qualities, educational background, work experience, and skill sets may all be considered by this technology. Because of this, recruiters are able to save time while also enhancing the quality of the applicants they provide to businesses.


Consequently, both the company and the applicant benefit from the acquisition of highly qualified individuals who are a better match for the open jobs. Reduced absenteeism and higher retention rates are direct results of this, because happy workers are more likely to show up for their shifts.


Time allocation


Filling out a workplace roster is a tedious and time-consuming process, but AI makes it much easier. Jobcart CEO Ajay Shokar claims that this function is revolutionary on its own.


In addition to identifying eligible candidates, Ajay claims that AI can predict which candidates would be most interested in accepting a job offer. "By leveraging machine learning, our applicant tracking systems are able to construct a roster that accurately anticipates both the availability and enthusiasm of potential employees for any particular shift."


Preventing fraud


The application of AI in payroll processing and fraud prevention is bringing it up to speed, accuracy, and security standards similar to those of online banking. A transaction is either approved or rejected by artificial intelligence depending on whether it is a "normal" one. 


To further reduce the likelihood of human mistakes, AI may check payroll records for discrepancies. Employee satisfaction rises and absenteeism falls as a consequence of a streamlined payroll process.


Biometric face recognition and digital ID verification both rely on AI to enhance workplace security and combat identity theft. Only those who have been verified by recruiters will be able to enter your workplace.


Minority Exclusion


We all have prejudices, whether we realize it or not. Employers as well. It is possible for unconscious prejudices to enter the talent acquisition process, even when we try very hard to avoid them.


By making screening and selection less reliant on human intervention, AI solutions may aid in the elimination of prejudice in the recruiting process. With the exception of predetermined prejudices, AI does not possess any inherent biases. What this implies is that AI-driven systems may screen applicants completely independently of demographic variables like age, gender, or race in order to find the most qualified ones.


Analytics that foretell future outcomes


AI can aid in the timely discovery of top talent, in addition to helping in the discovery of top talent overall. Artificial intelligence systems can sift through mountains of data collected from many sources, such as professional networks, social media, and job boards, to reveal patterns and trends in the employment market. Recruiters may use this information to anticipate changes in skill demand and adapt their hiring strategies appropriately. 


Staffing companies such as Jobcart are therefore able to respond quickly to company demands and handle times of high demand with little preparation. The system has probably already anticipated your requirement for a roster of 100 workers next week, Ajay explains.


Facilitating human-to-human assistance


While some may worry that AI will put them out of work, the truth is that technology will help recruiters do their jobs better, which is good news for businesses and job-seekers alike.


While Ajay acknowledges that AI will eliminate certain occupations, he also predicts that it will generate new ones.


By automating a lot of the mundane chores that recruitment teams have to do, AI is already revolutionizing the way we hire new employees. This frees up recruiters' brainpower to concentrate on other valuable tasks, including cultivating connections with customers and qualified applicants. 

In the end, staffing is still all about connecting individuals with employment opportunities.
blogs.pageTitle: Artificial Intelligence and Its Impact on the Employment Industry

blogs.blogTitle: Exploring the Pros and Cons of Employing Immigrants
blogs.blogDescp:
Companies who are having trouble filling positions will be pleased to hear the following from the most recent data released by Statistics Canada: An official government agency has said that in 2022, the number of persons immigrating to Canada reached a record high of over one million.


Immigration to Canada has a rich history; in fact, it was a tenet of the country's founding documents. Canadian immigration has always been a major economic driver, drawing millions of people from all over the world. Since 2015, when the federal government began to take a harder stance on immigration, that trend has only picked up speed. Canada has the highest number of landed immigrants or permanent residents among advanced economies, with about 25% of the population identifying as such, according to a study conducted in 2021.


Since there aren't enough people of working age to meet the demand for skilled labor, Canada's economy relies on increased immigration rates to fuel growth. Even while businesses are expanding, a large number of Baby Boomers are looking to exit the workforce. In this light, businesses that are in dire need of qualified workers to fill open positions benefit greatly from the flood of economic immigrants.


Increased immigration is good news for businesses looking for blue-collar workers, but there are also significant difficulties in integrating an immigrant workforce. 


Problems to Address


Dissimilarities in Culture


When integrating new immigrant staff, companies may face difficulties due to cultural differences that impact workplace norms and communication, increasing the likelihood of misunderstandings and disputes.


Difficulty Communicating Through Language


Workers whose first language is neither English or French may find it difficult to communicate with their employers. It might be challenging to provide directions, provide feedback, and guarantee work is done to the expected level when this happens. Frustration and lost productivity and safety on the job might result from employees' inability to effectively communicate with one another.


Obtaining Credentials and Recognition


It could be difficult for employers to confirm the credentials of lawful immigrants. This may make it harder to check whether employees have the right set of credentials to do their jobs well.


There is no way to overcome any of these obstacles. Employers can reap the advantages of immigrant workers and make sure they integrate well with native-born employees by taking a proactive approach that considers these possible challenges.


Benefits


More Varieties


A more diverse workforce has the potential to be more innovative, creative, and productive because of the unique views, opinions, and life experiences that each employee brings to the table.


Resolving the Deficit in Skill and Labor


More and more industries are experiencing a scarcity of available workers. To cover these gaps, businesses may take use of the immigrant workforce's abilities and adaptability, which can assist with both short-term and long-term staffing issues.


Encouraging Employees


Immigrants to Canada are ready to make a new life for themselves. This group of workers is very driven and will often sacrifice their own needs to meet deadlines. As a result of this work ethic, Canadians are even more devoted to workplace success than they already are.


Helping a Growing Population Age


As a larger proportion of the working-age population enters retirement, the Canadian workforce is becoming older. The influx of migrant workers helps to offset this tendency and guarantee that our expanding economy has access to sufficient labor.


Immigrants Help Our Economy Grow and Keep Our Schools Afloat


The demand for products and services is boosted by an infusion of new customers, which in turn stimulates local companies. Canadian colleges and universities are able to keep their academic programs competitive because to the tuition dollars sent by overseas students, even when fewer students from the host country are enrolling. That way, we know our employees will keep getting the training they need.


Benefits of Migrants on GDP


The economy benefits from migrants in general. A 1 percentage point rise in the ratio of migrants in the adult population raises GDP per person in advanced countries by up to 2 percent in the longer term, according to the International Monetary Fund (IMF). 


Rewards Await Those Ready to Take on the Obstacles


Immigration is a major factor in Canada's population boom. Businesses that rely on blue-collar workers will benefit greatly from this increase in the labor force. 


If businesses are aware of the possible obstacles to the seamless integration of permanent residents, temporary foreign workers, and international students, they can take proactive measures to eliminate or greatly reduce these problems before they even occur. The advantages clearly surpass the disadvantages. Those plans will revolve on technology.


Engaging a tech-savvy and digitally connected immigrant workforce will be made possible via social media and mobile communications. Companies can make the most of this amazing resource with the aid of employment services like Jobcart, which combines digital technologies with human knowledge. And that's great news for companies and immigrants to Canada.
blogs.pageTitle: Exploring the Pros and Cons of Employing Immigrants
blogs.pageKeywords: Exploring the Pros and Cons of Employing Immigrants
blogs.pageDescp: Exploring the Pros and Cons of Employing Immigrants

blogs.blogTitle: The Staffing Industry and the Effects of Vertical Market Dynamics
blogs.blogSummary: Experts in the field of personnel recruitment and management are brought together in the Jobcart Interview Series to talk about new ideas in the field.
blogs.blogDescp:
Experts in the field of personnel recruitment and management are brought together in the Jobcart Interview Series to talk about new ideas in the field. 


The impact of vertical market dynamics is discussed by Jobcart's CEO and co-founder Sumit Sahdev and Jobcart's Director of Sales and Business Development Eddie Diaz in this interview that is part of the Jobcart Interview Series.

A fast-growing sector of the economy is the one dealing with the logistics and fulfillment of online orders. The need for blue-collar workers to staff fulfillment centers and warehouses has skyrocketed in recent years due to the increasing number of prospective consumers who buy online. There are worries about a possible labor shortage as a result of this increase in demand, however, since there are too many enterprises competing for too few people.


A changing blue-collar environment


Historically, blue-collar workers were mostly employed by brick-and-mortar establishments. However, due to changes in business models and the increasing number of online shoppers, blue-collar workers are no longer required in retail but rather in warehouses and fulfillment centers to assist with processing and shipping consumer items bought online.


As a result of the increased demand, several industries are experiencing a scarcity of personnel to fill available roles. In places where there are a lot of warehouses and fulfillment facilities, this is a major issue. Consequently, businesses are trying to recruit workers by providing more flexible schedules, greater compensation, and other incentives.


A number of corporations in this industry are putting pressure on smaller businesses to abandon brick-and-mortar stores, according to Sahdev. "Since everyone is using the same pool of potential employees, we can expect a severe scarcity of laborers."


Deficits and shifts in regulation


The transportation sector is growing at a rapid pace as well. This sector is essential to the growth of online shopping as it establishes the networks that get products from suppliers to consumers' doorsteps. The transportation sector, however, has its own unique problems.


Diaz adds that although the sector is seeing high levels of activity, it is also encountering some obstacles. It's like supply and demand aren't on the same page. It's not there.


It is becoming more difficult for businesses to deliver products effectively due to the transportation industry's pressing demand for additional drivers. As a result, the whole supply chain may be affected by service delays and higher expenses. It is becoming more difficult for businesses to locate competent drivers due to new rules like the Electronic Logging Device (ELD) requirement.


Production returns to its roots


Manufacturing is another sector that is facing new and different kinds of workforce issues. There may be fewer production workers required on factory floors thanks to emerging technologies like AI and automation, but there is a greater need for qualified workers to run and maintain all that machinery.


Manufacturing in Canada is heavily dependent on nations outside of the country, with China serving as a key center. I feel like people are starting to realize how important it is to be able to take care of themselves. According to Sahdev. 


"There will be a need to hire more workers because the government is offering good incentives for businesses to-shore,'" meaning that they are seeking to bring production from third-party nations like China and elsewhere.


New challenges, new answers


Another sector hit hard by this vertical market is retail. Workers are required to assist with both customer support and product packaging and shipping as an increasing number of manufacturers shift to selling their wares online. Staffing strategies need to be updated to keep up with the ever-changing nature of current demand cycles.


"More workers and more worker flexibility are going to be required so that they can be accessible at all times. Diaz argues that the only option for retail giants to make their business models viable is to rely on contingent workers.


Commonalities and differences


The characteristics of vertical markets have a strong impact on the staffing business. The rising popularity of online shopping is causing major shifts in many different businesses, including transportation, storage, retail, e-commerce, and logistics. 

In order to maintain a steady supply of workers, it is necessary to address the demands of each of these interdependent industries, which in turn affect one another.


In order to overcome these obstacles, organizations need to be proactive when recruiting and retaining employees, collaborate with regulators to establish stable and growth-friendly regulations and investigate flexible staffing options to fulfill operational needs.


"Innovative problem-solving and proactive preparation are crucial for companies," Sahdev says in his conclusion.
blogs.pageTitle: The Staffing Industry and the Effects of Vertical Market Dynamics
blogs.pageKeywords: The Staffing Industry and the Effects of Vertical Market Dynamics
blogs.pageDescp: The Staffing Industry and the Effects of Vertical Market Dynamics

blogs.blogTitle: The Top 6 Reasons to Employ Temporary Workers
blogs.blogSummary: Military generals order reinforcements when they find themselves outnumbered in combat. They reduce their soldiers and fortify their supply lines after a victory. The value of flexible deployment is now being recognized by personnel managers as well.
blogs.blogDescp:
Military generals order reinforcements when they find themselves outnumbered in combat. They reduce their soldiers and fortify their supply lines after a victory. The value of flexible deployment is now being recognized by personnel managers as well.


Companies in the manufacturing and storage areas might gain a significant competitive edge by using temporary labor. A flexible staffing approach using temporary workers may assist in meeting variable productivity targets while managing payroll expenses, especially during the seasonal peak times of demand in certain areas.


Perks of Contract Employees


Employing temporary labor has six advantages for businesses in the industrial and warehousing sectors:


1. Limits Expenditures


Hiring temporary workers may help a business save money on personnel expenditures, which is good for their bottom line.


Employment benefits like health insurance, retirement plans, and paid time off are unnecessary for businesses that hire temporary workers on an as-needed basis. When compared to the expense of recruiting full-time personnel, this results in significant savings on recruitment and training.


The cost of employing a full-time employee, including benefits, may vary from 1.3 to 1.7 times the person's income, according to Jobcart, a prominent supplier of staffing services. A significantly more economical option is to hire temporary workers and pay them the agreed-upon hourly wage via the staffing agency.


2. Goes Up as Needed


The warehousing and industrial industries in North America tend to have seasonal peak periods. During times of high demand, permanent staff might be relieved of some of their workload by hiring temporary workers.


The capacity to adapt to fluctuating demand is a major benefit of hiring a temporary worker. As a result, businesses may avoid paying similar permanent employees extra to make up for seasonal workers' absence or to substitute for permanent employees' vacation or maternity leave, two major causes of workforce shortages.


Furthermore, temporary staffing is a cost-effective method of bringing in fresh workers for specific projects or short-term tasks. Companies may save up to 30% on labor expenditures by hiring temporary workers for short periods of time for seasonal work or temporary openings, as pointed out by Jobcart.


3. Gains Access to Skilled Employees


It may take some time to identify and train workers who possess the required skill sets. Only highly qualified, sector-specific temporary workers who can create an immediate impression are considered by staffing companies. 

Industrial and warehousing organizations may tap into a pool of qualified temporary workers who have already been through a screening process. Jobcart claims that this industry-specific hiring procedure will cut down on expenses associated with recruiting, advertising, and direct hiring, among other things.


4. Minimizes Dangers and Legal Obligations


Being well-versed in the rules and laws that pertain to payroll, taxes, and employee benefits may help you navigate the complicated and expensive world of compliance. Employers may alleviate some of this stress by forming partnerships with temporary staffing agencies, who take care of compliance risks and obligations.


In addition, most temp and staffing companies have liability insurance, which lowers your risk of having to pay out of pocket for lawsuits that may arise from workplace accidents or property damage.


5. Do a Test Run Before You Buy


Using a "temp-to-hire" trial term to evaluate candidates for permanent employment allows for a more thorough and precise evaluation of the candidates.


Businesses may get a good idea of a candidate's work ethic, abilities, and cultural fit from a temporary worker, and they can use this information to make a permanent hiring decision.


6. Helps Get the Most Done


Interim employees boost output. You might compare them to a top-notch special forces unit that, once airlifted, could make an instant effect in any scenario without undergoing basic training.


Temporary workers have experience and flexibility, and they are used to moving between companies with varied cultures and needs. As a result, less time is needed to train permanent employees. 

In addition, businesses see fewer, if any, operational delays or mistakes caused by incompetent employees when staffing agencies like Jobcart recruit temporary workers whose credentials match those of their clients.


Contemporary Obstacles, Contemporary Solutions


Changes are occurring in Canada's labor market. In response to the upheaval in the economy and the rapid development of new technologies, many companies have begun to use temporary workers as the backbone of their flexible employment plans. Workers are also undergoing transformations as a result of these shifts in corporate practices.


Jobcart reports that millennials and Gen Zers are less inclined to commit to long-term employment with one company and that this tendency will probably persist.


Millennial and Gen Z workers are able to achieve their work-life balance objectives with the help of a flexible staffing strategy, which also enables businesses access to the labor force needed to handle seasonal needs.


Rallying the Horsemen


In order to effectively manage the seasonal labor demands in the industrial and warehousing sectors, temporary and contract workers provide substantial benefits.


Employing temporary workers gives companies more leeway to scale up or down depending on demand, gives them access to a pool of qualified candidates who can be put to work quickly, lessens the likelihood of problems with regulatory compliance, and lets you test the waters with potential new hires before making a permanent offer.


The best part is that temporary workers help companies save money on staffing while still getting the most out of their workers. No matter the kind of battle you're fighting, having the right people on staff may make all the difference.
blogs.pageTitle: The Top 6 Reasons to Employ Temporary Workers
blogs.pageKeywords: The Top 6 Reasons to Employ Temporary Workers
blogs.pageDescp: The Top 6 Reasons to Employ Temporary Workers

blogs.blogTitle: 6 Ways to Enhance the Culture of a Blue Collar Workplace
blogs.blogSummary: A competitive advantage in today's work market is having happy employees. This is particularly the case in blue-collar fields where competition is fierce, including building, manufacturing, warehousing, and transportation.
blogs.blogDescp:
A competitive advantage in today's work market is having happy employees. This is particularly the case in blue-collar fields where competition is fierce, including building, manufacturing, warehousing, and transportation.


Finding and keeping good employees is the key to success for every blue-collar company. Finding and retaining excellent support may be challenging in blue-collar jobs due to the physically hard responsibilities, long hours, poor compensation, and restricted career progression options.


On the other hand, there are strategies to deal with and overcome this obstacle. Employers may do their part to make blue-collar workers feel appreciated and encouraged by implementing measures to build a welcoming and inclusive workplace. Furthermore, this contentment may significantly impact a business's financial line.

Six suggestions for fostering a more positive work environment:


1. Establish a Secure and Pleasant Workplace


Manual laborers, known as "blue-collar workers," must have access to a clean, safe workplace. It sends a message of concern for the employee's welfare, which may help with both hiring and keeping them around.


Providing workers with the right tools and equipment is an employer's top priority so that they can do their tasks well and safely. Properly constructed furniture, machinery that is regularly serviced, and, where needed, personal protective equipment (PPE) are all part of this.


Training employees properly on how to use equipment and to follow all safety protocols is an important step in improving worker safety. Training new recruits and providing ongoing re-training to current employees are both essential components of this. Preventing accidents and keeping the workplace safe requires regular inspections and maintenance.


2. Foster Strong Bonds With Others


Building a healthy work environment for blue-collar workers requires a focus on fostering strong connections. When employees are acknowledged and appreciated, they are more likely to be satisfied with their position and less likely to leave.


It is the responsibility of employers to provide avenues for workers to voice their opinions and proposals in an open and honest manner. In addition to measuring engagement and happiness, engagement surveys may identify problem areas.


A more pleasant work environment is the result of team-building exercises, which increase employee cooperation and foster friendship. Company trips, team challenges, and team meals are all great ways to foster camaraderie and unity among employees. Staff members will be more dedicated to the success of the business if they believe their contributions matter.


3. Provide Chances for Training and Development


They will invest back into you if you help them develop professionally. Showing blue-collar workers that their labor for your company will lead to more than just a paycheck by providing them with opportunities to learn new skills and climb the corporate ladder is a great way to keep them around.

In order to foster a growth mindset among their staff, employers should provide opportunities for on-the-job training, seminars for professional development, or even online courses. By expanding employees' knowledge and making their jobs more fulfilling, learning opportunities boost morale and productivity in the workplace. Everyone on staff, not just new hires, is subject to this.


Moreover, if you want to keep your best employees and attract new ones, you need to provide them with opportunities to advance in their careers. Workers are more invested in their jobs and less prone to look for possibilities elsewhere when they can understand how their work contributes to their professional growth.


4. Acknowledge and Incentives Employee Success


We are all seeking approval. Moreover, our morale plummets and productivity follows suit when we feel unappreciated. The morale and happiness of blue-collar workers may be greatly improved by incentive and recognition programs.


Award ceremonies, public acknowledgments, or personalized thank-you cards are some employee appreciation tactics that employers can think about using to honor and celebrate the accomplishments of blue-collar workers. Flexible work hours or extra paid time off are examples of non-monetary bonuses and perks that may enhance employee happiness, retention, and productivity.


Building a culture that encourages gratitude and loyalty among workers is possible when employers recognize and reward people for their efforts. Awards for employee of the month, milestone parties, and experiential incentives like spot bonuses for outstanding performance are all parts of recognition and reward programs.


5. Put Yourself in the Shoes of Blue-Collar Workers


Employees are humans first and foremost. Another important part of making the workplace comfortable for employees is getting to know and understand the personal and family life of blue-collar workers. This may be a great way to keep good employees around.


In order to help their workers successfully juggle their personal and professional lives, employers should foster an environment that encourages a good work-life balance. Some methods in which businesses may show they care about their workers' lives outside of work include allowing for more flexible scheduling, establishing regulations regarding parental leave, and offering tools to help with mental health.


Another option for businesses is to establish employee assistance programs (EAPs), where employees may get private counseling and other support services while they are going through tough times.


Get to know your employees; that's the surest method to demonstrate compassion for them. Get to know them by name. Inquire about their family life and home lifestyle. Discover what they like and don't like; if they're sports fans, for instance, you may connect over watching the game. Investing in people takes very little effort but may provide substantial gains in efficiency and loyalty.


6. Promote Staff Participation and Ownership


One certain way to boost morale in the office is to give blue-collar workers more say over their daily tasks. Workers who take pleasure in what they 'own' as an organization are more likely to be loyal to it.


By including their employees in decision-making, employers may foster an environment where employees feel valued and empowered to provide solutions to the problems faced by the organization. Taking this tack encourages workers to take ownership of their work and their accomplishments, which boosts morale and productivity.


To get management and staff opinions, you may use suggestion boxes, have town hall meetings, or form focus groups. Members of the workforce have a voice in the company's processes and results via joint health and safety committees.


Gaining an Advantage Over Your Rivals


The battle for blue-collar jobs is serious, and rightfully so. Having dependable, hard-working employees is crucial to the success of your organization. The good news is that you can take steps to make your company more attractive to potential employees and keep them there longer.


Having the people power you need to succeed can be achieved by prioritizing a safe and comfortable work environment, investing in training and development, fostering positive relationships, recognizing and rewarding achievements, showing empathy for personal lives, and encouraging employee involvement.


Your success is dependent on the contentment and joy of your staff. Keeping blue-collar workers interested, motivated, and devoted to their professions is possible when businesses provide an inclusive and supportive work environment. Your company's retention rate and financial success will both see significant improvements as a result of this.

blogs.pageKeywords: 6 Ways to Enhance the Culture of a Blue Collar Workplace
blogs.pageDescp: 6 Ways to Enhance the Culture of a Blue Collar Workplace

blogs.blogTitle: 7 Steps to Make Your Application Stand Out
blogs.blogSummary: Finding a job is a very competitive process. There are probably hundreds, if not thousands, of other people who share your enthusiasm for the position and have comparable skills and experience when you apply for a job. In what ways can you stand out?
blogs.blogDescp:
Finding a job is a very competitive process. There are probably hundreds, if not thousands, of other people who share your enthusiasm for the position and have comparable skills and experience when you apply for a job. In what ways can you stand out?


You'll have to make a good impression on more than simply recruiting managers. The Applicant Tracking System (ATS) is the first line of defense before a person views your application. These computer programs scan resumes for relevant terms and phrases and mark them as acceptable. A resume will never get beyond the screening process unless it contains those keywords.


Your application's next challenge, after passing the automated technology screening, is to captivate a human recruiting manager. Creativity, initiative, enthusiasm, likeability, and adaptability are some of the qualitative traits that individuals often use to evaluate others. You may put yourself ahead of the competition by emphasizing these qualities in addition to your objective credentials.


In the end, whether you get an interview or your CV gets tossed in the trash depends on how effectively you write your job application. To increase the likelihood that the appropriate person sees your application and hires you, please adhere to the seven useful tips provided below.


Personalize Your Application Materials


While it may seem like a lot of work, the first step in being hired is writing a unique cover letter for each job application. Sending out resumes that go unread is a waste of time compared to actually landing a job.


The certain method to go overlooked when searching for employment is to send out generic resumes and cover letters to every business. This "shotgun" technique does not work. Personalizing your application materials to meet the job needs demonstrates a thorough reading of the job description, emphasizes your relevant talents and experiences that align with the job requirements, and demonstrates your willingness to go above and beyond.


To tailor your application to a particular position, it is important to study the job description thoroughly to learn about the duties and skills needed for the position. Make mental notes of the precise words and phrases they use to characterize them. Examine your current résumé and cover letter in light of the position's specifications to see how you can best showcase your abilities and experience.


If a job posting calls for familiarity with a certain piece of software, for example, you should highlight your relevant expertise in your application, making sure to utilize the same language as the posting. Make yourself known as Gandalf if they need someone proficient in Excel.


Capitalize on Keywords Relevant to Your Industry


You have a better chance of getting the job if you know the terminology. One important tactic is to use terms that are relevant to the sector and the organization. With so many applications coming in, many organizations are turning to applicant tracking systems to sift through them all for certain terms.


With these terms sprinkled throughout your application, you improve your odds of passing the first round of screening and landing an interview. You may get industry-specific keywords by perusing corporate job advertisements online for comparable roles and making mental notes of the qualifications listed.


Exhibit Your Character


Demonstrating your personality may provide you an advantage over other applicants, even if recruiters and hiring managers are primarily interested in individuals with the necessary abilities. All sorts of personal and professional traits may fall under this category, including the way you communicate, your work ethic, your attitude, and any special abilities you may have, such as musical skill or athletic prowess. An online portfolio link would be a great addition if you have any artistic interests.


A well-written cover letter is an opportunity to showcase your character. Make use of your cover letter as an opportunity to introduce yourself and demonstrate how your character aligns with the requirements of the open job. For instance, if the position you're seeking calls for strong communication abilities, emphasize how your gregarious nature benefited your last employer.


Highlight Your Accomplishments


Exhibit more than only your employment credentials. Highlight your accomplishments on your resume. A company may see your worth from a list of talents, but what shows is how those skills transfer into real-world success via a sequence of well-presented accomplishments.


Make sure to utilize precise, measurable terms when describing your achievements. Substitute specific examples like "Implemented a new sales strategy that led to a 25% increase in monthly sales" with more generalized ones like "Increased sales."


Make the Most of Social Media


Social networking is a great tool for recruiters and hiring managers to research potential applicants before scheduling interviews. Maintaining a professional image online is essential. Improving your web presence is as easy as following a few guidelines:


Keep your social media accounts and usernames businesslike at all times.


  • Make sure your profiles are current.
  • Disseminate reliable industry news and expert analysis.
  • Take part in online discussions and interact with people in your professional network.


Help Out or Work Independently


Candidates who show initiative and dedication to their job are highly valued by employers. You may enhance your CV and cover letter by volunteering or taking on temporary jobs in your area of expertise. This will help you become identified by companies.


Volunteering also gives you the chance to meet other professionals in your sector, which might open doors to job prospects. Keep in mind that hard references from previous jobs and relevant experience from freelancing or voluntary work are great assets when applying for jobs or interviewing for them.


Continue with


Last but not least, follow-up after applying might help it stand out. The goal is to make it simple for a firm to recruit you, and one way to achieve so is to put yourself in their shoes. Sending a follow-up email or calling the hiring manager shows that you're committed to the role, that you're professional, and that you're interested in the job.


Follow these guidelines when contacting a potential employer about a fresh job application:


  • It would be polite to wait five working days before contacting the person by phone or email to follow up.
  • Maintain an air of professionalism and courtesy when you interview with the recruiting manager.
  • Inquire as to if there have been any developments on the post, and if not, convey your continued enthusiasm for the opportunity.


Diversify to advance


Competition for jobs is fierce in today's economy. You should look for methods to differentiate your application. Your chances of getting an interview for your dream job are little to none if your application seems like it belongs in the sea of other strong contenders.


Fortunately, there are a few easy things everyone can do to stand out to hiring managers... and their automated screeners.


Our suggestions aren't ground-breaking, and they aren't very challenging either. Using keywords to personalize cover letters and resumes is nothing new. It is simple to highlight your volunteer activities, accomplishments, and personality. Following up on applications isn't exactly ground-breaking, but keeping a businesslike social media presence is simple sense. However, you would be surprised by how many people who are looking for work do not follow these steps.


You may take advantage of other people's laziness and give yourself a leg up in the job market by taking the time to thoroughly research and apply for every position. This will increase your chances of finding your desired job.
blogs.pageTitle: 7 Steps to Make Your Application Stand Out
blogs.pageKeywords: 7 Steps to Make Your Application Stand Out
blogs.pageDescp: 7 Steps to Make Your Application Stand Out

blogs.blogTitle: Ontario's New Staffing Rules: 3 Ways They Impact You
blogs.blogSummary: New employment regulations under the Ontario government's "Working for Workers Act, 2021" will have far-reaching effects on businesses and individuals with employees in the province.
blogs.blogDescp:
New employment regulations under the Ontario government's "Working for Workers Act, 2021" will have far-reaching effects on businesses and individuals with employees in the province. 


To help you understand how the new Ontario staffing regulations will influence your business, we will go over the main points of the act, go over the proposed changes (such as licensing requirements for recruiters and staffing firms), clarify the ban on noncompete agreements, and talk about the 'disconnect' policies that deal with communications that take place after business hours.


Keeping up with the ever-changing legislation is crucial, as is taking the initiative to update employment and employee contracts to reflect the latest standards. Gaining a grasp of these developments will enable you to effortlessly adjust to the new standards, guaranteeing that your firm remains compliant while enjoying the possible advantages of a fairer and more competitive employment market.


1. A Guide to Staffing Firm Licensing

Businesses and employees alike will benefit from the Ontario government's new licensing rules, which will make it easier to track and prosecute negligent employees, employers, and staffing agencies.

A license is going to be necessary for staffing businesses and recruiters in Ontario to operate under the "Working for Workers Act, 2021”. These businesses must now get licenses to operate legally, which guarantees they will treat their temporary employees properly and adhere to all regulations pertaining to workplace safety. 


Businesses that rely on staffing firms to fill open positions will feel the effects of this firsthand. Fines and penalties may be imposed on client businesses who use employment agencies without the proper licensing. 


This rule is expected to be enforced by the government no later than 2024. Up until then, companies can start getting ready for the shift by taking stock of their present hiring practices and making sure they're collaborating with legitimate companies. 


Effect on Businesses That Employ Temporary Workers


Staffing agencies must now display a valid license in all contracts with their clients in order to comply with the new licensing requirements of the legislation. Indemnification agreements are another tool that client businesses use to shield themselves from legal action in the event that they hire an unregistered staffing agency. This extra safeguard makes sure that everyone is on the same page about the new rules and regulations and what they entail.


Companies should be careful when dealing with staffing agencies to make sure they follow the new rules. One option is to look for a list of registered staffing agencies on the official government website. Another is to ask prospective business associates for documentation of the company's legitimacy. Businesses may protect themselves from fines and make sure they're dealing with trustworthy employment agencies by following these steps.


2. Non-Compete Agreements Disallowed


Prohibiting non-compete agreements is a major move by the Ontario government towards creating a more transparent and competitive employment market. 


No longer may Ontario employers require their employees to sign non-compete clauses in their employment contracts as of October 25, 2021. (Working for Workers Act, 2021). This decision brings the province in line with other jurisdictions that have outlawed non-compete clauses in employment insurance, such as France and parts of California. 

Several firms will benefit from this non-compete restriction, but tech companies looking to relocate to Ontario stand to gain the most. Employees would be allowed to migrate between enterprises within the same sector without restrictions on non-compete, which would encourage teamwork and new ideas. A more competitive employment market, made possible by the removal of non-competes, may entice top talent to the area. 

In order to meet the requirements of this new rule, Ontario employers are advised to evaluate and revise their existing employment contracts.


3. Procedures for Terminating Communications Outside of Business Hours

The politicians in Ontario are reacting to the growing incursion of electronic communication into our private lives and leisure time via means like cell phones and email. Companies may help their workers avoid burnout by encouraging them to take breaks from work and establishing disconnect rules.


Businesses with 25 or more workers will need to put limitations on employee communications outside of work hours into a written policy as a result of the new law. Companies should have standards in place that spell out what workers should expect from them and how they should handle work-related messages outside of normal business hours. 


Even though there are no official rules regarding disconnect policies just yet, companies may start getting ready by taking stock of what they already have in place. June 2, 2022 was the cutoff date for businesses to implement their disconnect policy. Companies should be ready to change their practices to conform to government regulations when new information becomes available.


Opportunities and Threats Facing Companies


Companies and recruiters may have difficulties in adapting to the new rules. Possible difficulties include learning the ins and outs of the new regulations, allocating more resources to ensure compliance, and updating current procedures. On the other hand, these modifications might also bring about a number of advantages.

Employers will be held more accountable and hiring procedures will be made more transparent as a result of the "Working for Workers Act, 2021." All workers will benefit from these changes. Businesses and recruiters in Ontario may help make the employment market more fair and competitive by accepting the new rules. 


The region's businesses will be able to compete for top talent, and residents will be able to improve their skills and knowledge thanks to a more fair and competitive employment market. 


Keep Up-to-Date Moving Forward


Numerous stakeholders, including companies, staffing agencies, remote workers, and recruiters, will feel the effects of the "Working for Workers Act, 2021"'s revisions to Ontario legislation. In order to secure compliance and enjoy the possible advantages of a more fair and competitive labor market, stakeholders must be aware of these planned changes and adjust to the new standards. 


In light of the impending changes to employment legislation, it is critical for companies and recruiters to monitor developments closely and act swiftly to mitigate any potential consequences. Successfully navigating the "Working for Workers Act, 2021" and thriving in the ever-changing Ontario work market requires meticulous preparation and a dedication to comprehending the new requirements.
